LAW & MOTION, DEPT. 302, OCT-01-2024 HEARING HELD RE: DEFENDANT GENERAL MOTORS, LLC'S DEMURRER TO AMENDED COMPLAINT. THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT GENERAL MOTORS, LLC'S DEMURRER TO THE FIFTH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR FRAUDULENT CONCEALMENT WITHIN PLAINTIFF YOLANDA JONES' FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED. (SEE MINI-MINUTES FOR DETAILS.) COUNSEL FOR THE PREVAILING PARTY IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE AND SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: RICHARD B. ULMER JR.; CLERK: S. KANE; NOT REPORTED. (302/RBU)

MIL NO. 6 FILED BY DEFENDANT GENERAL MOTORS, LLC

LAW AND MOTION, 301, PLAINTIFFS YOLANDA JONES, LAMONT JOHNSON'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE DISMISSAL UNDER CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE SECTION 473(B) AND REINSTATE CASE IS DENIED. (SEE MINI MINUTES FOR COMPLETE RUILNG) DEFENDANT GENERAL MOTORS' UNTIMELY OPPOSITION PAPERS WERE NOT CONSIDERED. PLAINTIFFS FAIL TO OFFER ANY FACTS, LAW OR CIRCUMSTANCES THAT THEY DID NOT OR COULD NOT RAISE AT THE TIME THIS CASE WAS DISMISSED. PLAINTIFFS' FAILURE TO BE READY FOR A PROVE-UP HEARING IS NOT THE KIND OF MISTAKE THAT IS REMEDIABLE BY CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE 437(B) NOR DO PLAINTIFFS CITE ANY AUTHORITY SUGGESTING OTHERWISE. COUNSEL FOR THE PREVAILING PARTY IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE TENTATIVE RULING. VISTING JUDGE: HAROLD E. KAHN; CLERK: W. TRUPEK; NOT REPORTED (301/HEK)

Appearances via Zoom Conference
Aimee Stern, Esq., STRATEGIC LEGAL PRACTICES (310) 929-4900 astern@slpattorney.com Attorney for Plaintiffs, YOLANDA JONES and LAMONT JOHNSON
Arash Yaraghchian, Esq. ERSKINE LAW, APC (949) 777-6032 eservice-ca@erskinelaw.com ayaraghchian@erskinelaw.com Attorney for Defendant, GENERAL MOTORS LLC

Hearing held. ARGUED. Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFFS YOLANDA JONES, LAMONT JOHNSON'S Motion To Set Aside Dismissal Under Code Of Civil Procedure Section 473(B) And Reinstate Case.

Plaintiffs' motion to set aside dismissal is denied. Defendant General Motors' untimely opposition papers were not considered. Plaintiffs fail to offer any facts, law or circumstances that they did not or could not raise at the time this case was dismissed. Plaintiffs' failure to be ready for a prove-up hearing is not the kind of mistake that is remediable by Code of Civil Procedure 437(b) nor do plaintiffs cite any authority suggesting otherwise.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling.

Visting Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (301/HEK)

MOTION TO STRIKE Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-01-2024 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT GENERAL MOTORS, LLC'S MOTION TO STRIKE AMENDED COMPLAINT.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendant General Motors LLC's motion to strike plaintiff Yolanda Jones' request for punitive damages is denied. The first amended complaint sets forth a viable claim for fraudulent concealment, and Jones may recover punitive damages based on this claim. (Civ. Code 3294.)

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare and submit a proposed order in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: S. Kane; Not Reported. (302/RBU)

DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-01-2024 Overruled
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. BRIAN TAN, Esq. of Strategic Legal Practices, APC (Tel: 310-929-4900) for plaintiffs;
Atty. XYLON QUEZADA, Esq. of Erskine Law Group, PC (Tel: 949-777-6032) for defendant General Motors LLC.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT GENERAL MOTORS, LLC'S DEMURRER TO AMENDED COMPLAINT.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendant General Motors, LLC's demurrer to the fifth cause of action for fraudulent concealment within plaintiff Yolanda Jones' first amended complaint is overruled.

Jones purchased a 2020 Chevrolet Silverado with an allegedly defective 8-speed transmission that caused problems such as harsh shifts, jerking, and lurching. Jones sued GM for Song-Beverly Act violations and asserted one fraud claim. GM now demurs to the fifth cause of action for fraudulent inducement by concealment.

A claim for fraudulent concealment requires (1) concealment of a material fact, (2) while under a duty to disclose, (3) with intent to defraud plaintiff, (4) who was unaware of the fact and would have acted otherwise if she had known, (5) and who sustained damages. (Boschma v. Home Loan Center, Inc. (2011) 198 Cal.App.4th 230, 248.)

First, GM argues that Jones fails to plead fraud with the requisite specificity. But less specificity is required where, as here, the facts lie more in the knowledge of the opposite party. (Alfaro v. Community Housing Improvement System & Planning Assn., Inc. (2009) 171 Cal.App.4th 1356, 1384.) Here, Jones has alleged that GM was aware, from internal technical bulletins and other sources not available to consumers, that vehicles equipped with this 8-speed transmission displayed the same defects. (FAC para. 61-63.) GM allegedly concealed these defects when it marketed to consumers the defects were "normal," and attempted to fix Jones' vehicle despite its inability to do so. (FAC 65, 69.) Further, Jones alleges intent to induce reliance, justifiable reliance, and damages. (FAC 72, 64, 68, 73, 76.) Given the facts lie more within GM's knowledge, these allegations suffice to show fraudulent concealment. (Dhital v. Nissan North America, Inc. (2022) 84 Cal.App.5th 828, 844 [vehicle buyer pleaded viable concealment claim against manufacturer for concealing defects (review granted)].)

Second, GM argues that Jones fails to plead the requisite transactional relationship giving rise to a duty to disclose because she purchased the car from a dealer, not from GM. At the pleadings stage, however, allegations that a car was purchased from an authorized dealership and agent of the manufacturer, and that the manufacturer backed the car with an express warranty, can suffice. (Dhital, supra, 84 Cal.App.5th at 844.) Here, Jones adequately pleads such a transactional relationship based on GM's express warranties and her dealings with GM's authorized representative dealer and repair facilities. (FAC para. 11, 14, 65, 69, 75; Dhital, supra, 84 Cal.App.5th 828, 844.)

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare and submit a proposed order in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: S. Kane; Not Reported. (302/RBU)
